2026-04-09T20:42:32.8353098Z [36;1m# Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)[0m
2026-04-09T20:42:32.8353523Z [36;1m# Mask immediately to prevent timing vulnerabilities[0m
2026-04-09T20:42:32.8353922Z [36;1mAPI_KEY=$(openssl rand -base64 45 | tr -d '/+=')[0m
2026-04-09T20:42:32.8354237Z [36;1mecho "::add-mask::${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-09T20:42:32.8354477Z [36;1m[0m

Since this server uses SSE for a persistent connection, I need to figure out how to maintain that session - maybe by chaining multiple requests together or using the Streamable HTTP transport format that the newer protocol supports.

Review `sdk/core/Azure.Core/CHANGELOG.md` — verify the 1.53.0-beta.1 `Features Added` section and move the credential schema merge entry from `Other Changes` to `Features Added`\n2.
